question.text: What is the TDS rate on IDCW paid to NRI investors?

answer.text: 10% for equity schemes, 20% for debt schemes

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TDS on IDCW paid to NRI investors is 20% (plus applicable surcharge and cess), regardless of the scheme type or amount. Unlike resident investors, there is no Rs.5,000 threshold exemption for NRIs — TDS is deducted on every rupee of IDCW. **The Trap:** 10% is the resident investor TDS rate, not NRI. Candidates sometimes think equity and debt schemes have different IDCW TDS rates for NRIs — they don't. The 20% rate is uniform across all scheme types for NRIs. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers NRI taxation; Series II-B specifically tests the RTA's role in identifying NRI investors and applying the correct (higher) TDS rate. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NRI clients receiving IDCW lose 20%+ upfront to TDS. Recommend growth option instead — no TDS on unrealized gains, and they pay tax only on redemption. This improves compounding efficiency significantly for NRI investors.
